product.name: ArGiyim 290
product.categorySourceGroup: Ultra Durable, Ultra Breathable Front Side Polyester Back Side Cotton, PolyCotton Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: 80% Polyester 20% Cotton PolyCotton High Visible Water Repellent Satin Fabric
product.specLine: 290 g/m², 80% Polyester 20% Cotton High Visible, Chemical Resistant, 4/1 Satin Woven Fabric
descriptions: 80% Polyester 20% Cotton PolyCotton high visible, chemical resistant satin fabric. The front side of the fabric is 100% Polyester which is ultra durable for work conditions and light, the back side of the fabric is cotton which provides breathability to the user.
ArGiyim²90 are used for heavy outdoor jackets, safety jackets, pans and trousers.
Certified for EN ISO 20471 and for EN 13034
80% Polyester 20% Cotton PolyCotton High Visible Water Repellent Satin Fabric
290 g/m², 80% Polyester 20% Cotton High Visible, Chemical Resistant, 4/1 Satin Woven Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp > 2000N ; Weft > 800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp > 35N ; Weft > 30N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit certification to EN ISO 20471 and titling/description as high-visibility safety fabric for safety jackets and trousers directly supports hi-vis classification.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Heavy outdoor jacket/workwear use could theoretically apply to uniformed services but no explicit military or police reference exists.
Chemical resistance and EN 13034 certification suggest protective outdoor workwear but no explicit welding, foundry or metal-processing use is stated.

evidence: used for heavy outdoor jackets, safety jackets, pans and trousers
High Visible Water Repellent Satin Fabric
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly titled and certified as a high-visibility fabric (EN ISO 20471) intended for safety jackets and trousers, making hi-vis the clear, dominant application.

evidence: heavy outdoor jackets, safety jackets, pans and trousers
secondary.rationale: Beyond hi-vis certification, the fabric is designed for durable, chemical-resistant work garments (jackets/trousers/pants), consistent with general industrial workwear use as a secondary application.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Chemical resistance and EN 13034 relate to chemical protection, not explicit welding, foundry or metal-processing use.
No explicit military, police or tactical use stated in the product data.
EMI property present but no explicit electrical/high-voltage utility use stated; EN 1149/EMI alone insufficient.
